questions.question: A circle is divided into 4 equal parts and 1 part is shaded. What fraction is shaded?
questions.explanation: 1 shaded part out of 4 equal parts = 1/4. The bottom number (denominator) is the total number of equal parts.

questions.question: Which fraction represents 1 whole?
questions.explanation: When the numerator (top) equals the denominator (bottom), the fraction equals 1 whole. So 4/4 = 1.

questions.question: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Tim ate 3 slices. What fraction of the pizza did he eat?
questions.explanation: Tim ate 3 slices out of 8 total slices = 3/8. The numerator is the number eaten, the denominator is the total.

questions.question: A ribbon is divided into 3 equal parts. What fraction does ONE part represent?
questions.explanation: 1 part out of 3 equal parts = 1/3. This is called a unit fraction — it always has 1 on top.

questions.question: Which fraction is closest to 0 on a number line?
questions.explanation: 1/8 is closest to 0. The larger the denominator with the same numerator of 1, the smaller the fraction.

questions.question: What is 1/2 + 1/2?
questions.explanation: 1/2 + 1/2 = 2/2 = 1 whole. When the numerator equals the denominator, it equals 1.

questions.question: Which fraction equals 2/4?
questions.explanation: 2/4 = 1/2. Both show the same amount — exactly half. These are called equivalent fractions.

questions.question: How many fourths (1/4 pieces) are in 1 whole?
questions.explanation: There are 4 fourths in 1 whole. If you cut something into 4 equal pieces, you need all 4 pieces to make a whole.

questions.question: What is 1/4 + 1/4?
questions.explanation: 1/4 + 1/4 = 2/4 = 1/2. Adding fractions with the same denominator: just add the numerators.

questions.question: On a number line from 0 to 1, where is the fraction 1/2?
questions.explanation: 1/2 is exactly halfway between 0 and 1. It divides the number line into two equal parts.

questions.question: Which fraction is GREATER: 3/4 or 3/8?
questions.explanation: 3/4 is greater. When numerators are the same, the fraction with the smaller denominator is bigger (4 < 8, so each piece is larger).

questions.question: A pan of brownies is cut into 6 equal pieces. Maria ate 2 pieces. What fraction did she eat?
questions.explanation: Maria ate 2 pieces out of 6 total = 2/6. (Note: 2/6 is the same as 1/3, but 2/6 shows the exact pieces.)

questions.question: Which fraction equals 1/2?
questions.explanation: 2/4 = 1/2. Both represent exactly half. 2 out of 4 is the same as 1 out of 2.

questions.question: On a number line from 0 to 1, is 3/8 closer to 0 or closer to 1?
questions.explanation: 3/8 is closer to 0. Since 1/2 = 4/8, and 3/8 < 4/8, the fraction is less than halfway and therefore closer to 0.

questions.question: A rope is divided into 4 equal parts and 3 parts are shaded. What fraction is NOT shaded?
questions.explanation: If 3 out of 4 parts are shaded, then 1 part is not shaded. 1 out of 4 = 1/4.

questions.question: Which list shows fractions in order from LEAST to GREATEST?
questions.explanation: 1/8 < 1/4 < 1/2. Unit fractions with bigger denominators are smaller (the pieces are smaller when you cut into more parts).

questions.question: Which fraction is greater: 5/6 or 5/8?
questions.explanation: 5/6 is greater. The numerators are the same (both are 5), so compare denominators: 6 < 8, meaning each 1/6 piece is bigger than each 1/8 piece.

questions.question: Lena colored 2/3 of a rectangle. Ben colored 4/6 of the same size rectangle. Who colored more?
questions.explanation: They colored the same amount! 2/3 = 4/6. These are equivalent fractions — they both represent the same point on a number line.

questions.question: What is 1/4 + 1/4 + 1/4?
questions.explanation: 1/4 + 1/4 + 1/4 = 3/4. When adding fractions with the same denominator, add only the numerators: 1+1+1=3, denominator stays 4.

questions.question: What fraction of a week is 2 days? (A week has 7 days.)
questions.explanation: 2 days out of 7 days in a week = 2/7. The denominator is the total (7 days), the numerator is the part (2 days).

questions.question: A garden is divided into 8 equal sections. Tomatoes take 3 sections and carrots take 2 sections. What fraction of the garden is left for corn?
questions.explanation: Sections used: 3 + 2 = 5. Sections left: 8 − 5 = 3. Corn gets 3/8 of the garden.

questions.question: Which comparison is TRUE?
questions.explanation: 3/4 > 2/4 is true. Same denominator: bigger numerator = bigger fraction. 3 > 2, so 3/4 > 2/4.

questions.question: On a number line from 0 to 1, which fraction is located between 1/2 and 1?
questions.explanation: 3/4 is between 1/2 and 1. 3/4 = 0.75, which is between 0.5 (= 1/2) and 1.0.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 8 books. Sam has read 5 of them. What fraction has Sam NOT read yet?
questions.explanation: Books not read: 8 − 5 = 3. Fraction not read: 3/8.

questions.question: Which set of fractions is in order from GREATEST to LEAST?
questions.explanation: 3/4, 2/4, 1/4 — all have the same denominator (4), so bigger numerator = bigger fraction: 3 > 2 > 1.

questions.question: What is 1/3 + 2/3?
questions.explanation: 1/3 + 2/3 = 3/3 = 1 whole. Add the numerators: 1 + 2 = 3. The denominator stays 3. And 3/3 = 1.

questions.question: A pizza was cut into 8 equal slices. Jan ate 1/2 of the pizza. How many slices did Jan eat?
questions.explanation: 1/2 of 8 slices = 4 slices. Half of 8 is 4.

questions.question: Which fraction is equivalent to 4/8?
questions.explanation: 4/8 = 1/2 = 2/4. Divide both numerator and denominator by 2: 4÷2=2, 8÷2=4. So 4/8 = 2/4.

questions.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. 2/6 are shaded blue and 3/6 are shaded red. What fraction is unshaded?
questions.explanation: Shaded parts: 2/6 + 3/6 = 5/6. Unshaded: 6/6 − 5/6 = 1/6.

questions.question: Mia jogged 3/4 of a mile and then walked 1/4 of a mile. How far did she travel in all?
questions.explanation: 3/4 + 1/4 = 4/4 = 1 whole mile. When you add fractions with the same denominator: 3+1=4, keep the denominator 4, and 4/4 = 1.
